headline: Selling Pressure Sweeps the Board as Geopolitical Anxiety and Earnings Disappointments Define Thursday's Session
body_html:
Thursday's session closed on a defensive note, with declining issues outnumbering advancers by a ratio of roughly 308 to 189 across a 501-name universe. Semiconductors and software bore the steepest losses — down 1.45% and 1.38% respectively — while homebuilders and consumer names were not far behind, each surrendering more than 1.3%. Only Healthcare managed to finish in positive territory, eking out a 0.54% gain as investors sought refuge in life-sciences and contract-research names amid a broadly risk-off tone.

The macro backdrop provided little comfort. Geopolitical tensions linked to Iran dominated the newswire — reports of missile materiel transfers to Houthi forces and a flurry of congressional votes over war powers created a ceiling on risk appetite, while an oil-price surge added cost pressure across the consumer and industrial complex. A major grocery chain's warning that softening household spending will weigh on its full-year results reinforced the cautious read on the American consumer.

Thursday's standout mover was Gentherm, the automotive thermal-management supplier, which surged nearly 27% — an outsized single-session gain that underscored how sharply earnings surprises can cut through a down-tape. The broader lesson from today: when the macro dominates, stock selection and catalyst discipline remain the clearest path to alpha.

briefs.headline: Gentherm Rockets 27% in a Sea of Red
briefs.body: Gentherm posted the session's most dramatic move, surging 26.84% to lead the entire universe on Thursday. The automotive thermal-management supplier's outsized gain stood in stark contrast to broad market weakness, pointing to a catalyst-driven earnings or guidance beat that overwhelmed the negative macro tide.

briefs.headline: Medpace and Thermo Fisher Lift Healthcare to the Day's Only Green Sector
briefs.body: Healthcare finished as Thursday's sole advancing sector, rising 0.54%, powered in part by a 14.32% jump in contract-research firm Medpace and an 8.75% advance in life-sciences giant Thermo Fisher. The defensive character of the group attracted rotational flows as investors shed higher-risk cyclicals.

briefs.headline: Tesla Slides Nearly 14%, Deepening Auto-Tech's Rough Stretch
briefs.body: Tesla ended Thursday down 13.72%, the sharpest percentage decline among the day's notable movers. The sell-off extended pressure on the auto-tech segment and contributed meaningfully to the session's negative breadth, with no immediate catalyst visible to arrest the slide heading into Friday.

briefs.headline: Iran Tensions Drive Oil Higher, Weigh on Wall Street
briefs.body: A surge in crude prices compounded equity market unease on Thursday as multiple reports detailed IRGC commander movements and weapons transfers to Houthi forces in Yemen. Energy shares fell 0.39%, and broader risk sentiment deteriorated as congressional war-powers votes underscored the fluid geopolitical situation surrounding Iran.

briefs.headline: Grocery Giant's Profit Warning Flags Consumer Fatigue
briefs.body: Albertsons told investors Thursday that weaker grocery spending is set to compress both sales and earnings, sending its shares sharply lower. The warning resonated across the consumer sector, which dropped 1.33% on the session, reinforcing concerns that household budgets remain under meaningful pressure.

briefs.headline: AI-Themed ETFs Multiply Despite a Bruising Quarter for Tech
briefs.body: A JPMorgan report released Thursday documented a dramatic proliferation of artificial-intelligence-focused exchange-traded funds, even as the broader technology sector endured a difficult quarter. The finding underscores sustained investor appetite for AI exposure through diversified vehicles, even when individual-name volatility runs high.
